sol_accord.name: Sol Accord
sol_accord.description: A political and economic alliance formed by the major governments of the Sol System.
sol_accord.history: Formed during the early colonization of Mars to coordinate interplanetary development and maintain political stability between Earth, Luna, and the emerging colonies.
sol_accord.goals: Maintain stability and coordinate interplanetary expansion.

mars_frontier_coalition.name: Mars Frontier Coalition
mars_frontier_coalition.description: A coalition of Martian settlements advocating planetary autonomy.
mars_frontier_coalition.culture: Frontier engineering culture with strong local identity.
mars_frontier_coalition.history: Emerging political bloc among Martian colonies seeking greater self-governance within the Sol Accord political structure.
mars_frontier_coalition.goals: Increase political independence of Martian settlements.

lm_lines.name: LM Lines
lm_lines.description: LM Lines (Luna–Mars Lines) was a medium-sized private interplanetary transport company operating regular ferry traffic between lunar orbit and Mars during the Race for Sol period.
lm_lines.culture: Professional transport crews and civilian migration culture. Voyages were culturally regarded as routine long-distance journeys similar to a modest cruise or a long bus ride through space.
lm_lines.history: Founded in 2184 as a privately operated ferry service supported by infrastructure funding from Joined Lunar Command. The company transported settlers, workers, and infrastructure modules from lunar Lagrange transfer stations to Mars settlements using scheduled Hohmann transfer trajectories. As Mars developed its own transport infrastructure and shipyards, demand for lunar-based ferry services declined. LM Lines eventually declared bankruptcy around 2270.
lm_lines.goals: Provide reliable passenger and infrastructure transport between lunar orbit and Mars settlements.
